color.$description: background/surface/foreground/border stay independently-authored neutrals (no hue to match, so no cross-theme derivation needed). Every hued token (accent/accent-active/analogous-0..2/focus-ring/link/link-hover/success/warning/danger + -active) is derived from dark.json's actual color at the same hue/saturation, with lightness reduced until it clears ~7:1 (base tones) or ~5:1 (active/hover tones) contrast against white — literal hex, computed with libs/shared/design-tokens's HSL-preserving contrast solver, not re-derived at build time. accent/accent-active/focus-ring/link/link-hover are the new orange brand hue (from dark.json's literal #f26b22), replacing an earlier gold/amber derivation that visually collided with warning. warning/warning-active follow warningRamp's hue rotation to yellow (~50°) in core/color.json, keeping it distinct from the orange accent.

surface.$description: {color.neutral.100} — a soft, warm off-white one step down from background, not a flat pure grey. Matches dark.json's surface (#32302a, also drawn from the warm neutral family relative to its own background) instead of an unrelated cold-grey #e3e3e3 that read as muddy/disconnected next to a pure-white background. Used for chip-like chrome that WANTS to read as distinct from the page (badge's neutral tone, button's secondary variant) — NOT for Card/Material's resting background, which uses surface-plain instead (see below).

surface-plain.$description: Per docs/design-research/elevation-surface-case-study.md: the 'plain' base that Card/Material's resting (tier-0) background and --cr-color-surface-1..5's mixing base derive from — deliberately identical to `background` in light theme (a card at rest is flush with the page; only --cr-shadow-N communicates elevation, no fill or tint difference at all). Kept as its own semantic key rather than aliased to `background` directly so dark.json can point it at a different value (surface, not background) without touching every consumer's var() reference.

surface-tint.$description: As of docs/design-research/elevation-surface-case-study.md, only used for Card's interactive-hover state (see card.css) — light-theme --cr-color-surface-1..5 are flat aliases to --cr-color-surface (no tint; elevation reads via --cr-shadow-N alone, see build.mjs's appendDerivedSurfaceTokens). A PALE, high-lightness step of the accent hue, not a neutral. Same S/L pattern as the prior tints, re-hued to match the orange accent.
